title: Molten Ethiopian Berbere Stew
description: A vibrant, flavorful berbere stew featuring thyme and a touch of smokiness from the robata grill. This dish is perfect for a low-calorie dinner or lunch.

ingredients.name: Thyme
ingredients.notes: Fresh thyme is essential for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Any color bell pepper will work, but red adds a sweet pop of color.

ingredients.name: Tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Canned or fresh, whichever is convenient.

ingredients.name: Vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium broth is best for this dish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper is a must.

ingredients.name: Feta cheese (optional)
ingredients.notes: Crumble the feta on top for a creamy, tangy element.

ingredients.name: Scallions (optional)
ingredients.notes: Chop the scallions and add them as a garnish.

instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they soften and caramelize, about 20 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the berbere spice mix and thyme, and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant.
Add the red bell pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until it begins to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the stew has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
If using, crumble the feta cheese on top of the stew and garnish with chopped scallions.
